sourceMovie.title: [REC]
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: [REC] works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where [REC] excels in horror, The Ring (2002) doubles down on mystery to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Sinister
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Sinister (2012) is the natural next step after [REC]: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and mystery elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Blair Witch Project
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of [REC] who respond to its horror will find The Blair Witch Project (1999) immediately familiar. Both films share a mystery sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Taking of Deborah Logan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason [REC] fans keep landing on The Taking of Deborah Logan (2014) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Day of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Day of the Dead draws from the same horror well as [REC], channeling mystery in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1985 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Hell House LLC
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hell House LLC (2015) captures the horror appeal of [REC] without simply imitating it. The mystery streak running through both films is what puts Hell House LLC at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Legend of Hell House
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of [REC] — that dark edge, the mystery — translates cleanly into The Legend of Hell House (1973). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind 10 Cloverfield Lane (2016) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps [REC] rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Deliver Us from Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Deliver Us from Evil (2014) is the kind of film [REC] fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: [REC]²
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: [REC]² shares [REC]'s gift for horror — the 2009 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after [REC]?
faq.answer: Try The Ring, Sinister, The Blair Witch Project—each shares DNA with [REC] while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is [REC]?
faq.answer: [REC] (2007) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is [REC] part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "[REC] Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
